title: 500 Error Page with User Feedback Form and Slack Notification
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to describe what I was doing when an error occurred, so that the development team can diagnose and fix issues faster with real-time context.
description:
Enhance the production 500 error page (from MBT-317) with a user feedback form. When a 500 error occurs, the user sees a branded error page with a text input where they can describe what they were doing. On submission, the user's context, IP address, and user info are sent to a Slack channel for near real-time notification.

## User-Facing Message

**Oops! An Error Occurred.**
We have logged the issue and a ticket on our side. What would help us a ton is context about what you were doing to land on this issue! Thanks in advance for your help to make DataTools error free!

## Behavior

- The 500 status code is hidden from the user.
- A text input form allows the user to describe their actions.
- On submit, user info, IP address, and feedback are sent to the `#datatoolsystemalerts` Slack channel.
- Page is responsive (desktop and mobile).
acceptance_criteria: 500 error page displays a branded message without showing error codes
A text input form is available for users to describe what they were doing
Submitting the form sends user info, IP address, and feedback to Slack
The Slack notification posts to the #datatoolsystemalerts channel
The error page is responsive on mobile devices
The 500 status code is not visible to end users
ux_changes: Branded 500 error page with feedback form. Hides technical error details. Shows friendly message asking users to describe their actions. Responsive layout for mobile and desktop.
middleware_changes: Error page form submission triggers a Slack API notification with user context, IP address, and feedback text. Integrated with Slack webhook for the alerts channel.
implementation_notes: Initially configured for #datatoolsystemalerts channel but Slack returned channel-not-found. Temporarily using slackapitest channel. The error page was refined based on product owner feedback to hide the '500 Error' label and use a friendlier message. Page is responsive for mobile.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the feedback form with Slack integration but encountered channel-not-found errors for the target channel. Used test channel as fallback. Product owner requested hiding the 500 error label and provided specific user-facing copy. Developer updated the content and verified responsive layout on both desktop and mobile.
